reasoning: Provide a concise summary of the given passage, emphasizing the council’s actions, rationale, timelines, and budget without introducing new details.
output: The cabinet voted to remove the dangerous section of the Grade II pier from the pavilion to the seaward end, saying it could not wait for consent due to the risk of further collapse. Work on the dangerous structure is expected to take about three weeks. Parts of the Grade II pier, already closed after it gave way on 1 February, will be saved, catalogued, and stored. Iwan Davies, the council’s chief executive, said dismantling the structure is dangerous. Up to £650,000 has been set aside for the work, but final costs are unknown.
